text.title: Mutation Testing Bootstrap Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the Mutation Testing Bootstrap methodology. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Pick the canonical maintained mutation-testing tool per language: mutmut (Python) / stryker (JS/TS) / infection (PHP) / pitest (Java/JVM). Forks or alphas are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Tool drift produces unreproducible results; staying on canonical tools keeps the integration low-cost.

rule.statement: PR-scope mutation runs only mutate files changed in the PR (`git diff --name-only`). Full-repo mutation is reserved for nightly batch jobs.
rule.rationale: Full-repo mutation blows the CI budget; PR-scope keeps incremental feedback under 10 minutes.

rule.statement: Survivor rate ≤25% to start; ratchet -5% per quarter until 5% floor or budget breaks. PRs that raise survivor rate above gate fail the job.

rationale.source: Pitest + Stryker community recommendation; CSE empirical studies

rule.rationale: Without a gate, mutation results are observational and ignored.

rule.statement: PR-scope mutation MUST complete in ≤10 minutes. If exceeded, sample mutants (random or class-based) or shard.
Mutation testing requires green and stable baseline (zero flaky tests on three consecutive runs). Otherwise survivors are indistinguishable from flakes.
rule.rationale: Flaky tests confound mutation results; survivors get blamed on flakes and ignored.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent's context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-mutation-testing-bootstrap.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
